Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Lemonade by AMD: a fast and open source local LLM server using GPU and NPU 446 upvotes, 97 comments
  2. George Goble has died 113 upvotes, 21 comments
  3. Prefer do notation over Applicative operators when assembling records (2024) 23 upvotes, 2 comments
  4. Google releases Gemma 4 open models 1155 upvotes, 343 comments
  5. Significant progress made on Xbox 360 recompilation 68 upvotes, 15 comments
  6. Decisions that eroded trust in Azure – by a former Azure Core engineer 304 upvotes, 94 comments
  7. Qwen3.6-Plus: Towards real world agents 430 upvotes, 150 comments
  8. LinkedIn is searching your browser extensions 1564 upvotes, 683 comments
  9. C89cc.sh – standalone C89/ELF64 compiler in pure portable shell 19 upvotes, 3 comments
  10. Memo: A language that remembers only the last 12 lines of code 22 upvotes, 4 comments
  11. JSON Canvas Spec (2024) 82 upvotes, 28 comments
  12. Post Mortem: axios NPM supply chain compromise 15 upvotes, 2 comments
  13. Inside Nepal's Fake Rescue Racket 253 upvotes, 114 comments
  14. Artemis II's toilet is a moon mission milestone 138 upvotes, 58 comments
  15. Sweden goes back to basics, swapping screens for books in the classroom 742 upvotes, 381 comments
  16. 'Backrooms' and the Rise of the Institutional Gothic 172 upvotes, 80 comments
  17. A Few Good Magazines From the 70s and 80s 21 upvotes, 5 comments
  18. Good ideas do not need lots of lies in order to gain public acceptance (2008) 158 upvotes, 74 comments
  19. Magic the Gathering Deck Shuffler 41 upvotes, 14 comments
  20. Tailscale's new macOS home 327 upvotes, 170 comments
  21. Significant raise of reports 280 upvotes, 147 comments
  22. Show HN: Made a little Artemis II tracker 33 upvotes, 11 comments
  23. Artemis computer running two instances of MS outlook; they can't figure out why 322 upvotes, 236 comments
  24. Queueing Requests Queues Your Capacity Problems, Too 17 upvotes, 6 comments
  25. Maze Algorithms (1997) 11 upvotes, 3 comments
  26. Cursor 3 287 upvotes, 245 comments
  27. OpenAI Acquires TBPN 148 upvotes, 128 comments
  28. Tor Alva: The Tallest 3D-Printed Building in the World 6 upvotes, 2 comments
  29. The beginning of programming as we'll know it? 17 upvotes, 13 comments
  30. ParadeDB (YC S23) Is Hiring Database Internal Engineers (Rust) 0 upvotes, 0 comments
